mono.purpose: Every live numeric value: weight, reps, RPE, timers, 1RM.

motion.name: border-shift
motion.purpose: Icon/input hover and focus border color transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per screen (START / LOG / Build). Zero radius plus a 45deg-clipped top-right corner, the one signature shape in the system.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #FF4A1C; color: #0B0C0E; border: 0; padding: 15px 32px 15px 15px; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; clip-path: polygon(0 0, calc(100% - 16px) 0, 100% 16px, 100% 100%, 0 100%); transition: opacity .12s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: .92; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2.5px solid #FF4A1C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tag / Status Chip
components.description: Neutral outline tag used for exercise type, week-day status (done/alt/skipped/next). Recolors border+text by state; never filled except the current row.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Spline Sans Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .1em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 2px 6px; border: 1px solid #343A44; color: #8B9098; } .ds-tag--next { color: #FF4A1C; border-color: #FF4A1C; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Base container: flat, zero radius, one tonal step above the page background, hairline border.

components.name: Why-Block (Active, first-class)
components.description: The promoted why-line for the current exercise on the Active screen. Full border on all sides, never a side-stripe accent.
components.html: You cleared 57.5 kg every set last session at RPE 7. This is the next step up.
components.css: .ds-whyblock { display: block; box-sizing: border-box; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid #343A44; background: #1A1D23; color: #F4F5F6; font-size: 13px; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; }

narrative.overview: This is not a lifestyle app, it's a tool used mid-set, one-handed, under time pressure, in a gym. Every screen is designed to be glanced at and understood in under a second, then put back down. The aesthetic is dark, angular, numeric: tabular-nums everywhere a number appears, zero corner radius, no shadows, no gradients. It reads like cockpit instrumentation, not a consumer wellness product. This system explicitly rejects the generic fitness-influencer look: no gradient hero cards, no badge/streak-shaming, no rounded-friendly SaaS softness, no hype copy, no decorative motivational photography.
keyCharacteristics: Dark canvas by default, zero corner radius, no shadows or blurs
Exactly one accent color per screen, spent on the single next action
All numeric data set in tabular-nums monospace so values don't jitter as they change
Status is quiet (stroke-check, hairline circle); only the one active thing is loud
A single 45-degree-clipped corner is the one signature shape, echoing the logo stencil cut

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Signal Orange appears on exactly one element per screen: the single most important next action. Status, progress, and everything else uses neutral or the semantic status hues, never orange for decoration.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Any number the user reads at a glance during a set, weight, reps, RPE, rest countdown, est. 1RM, renders in tabular-nums Spline Sans Mono. Prose never gets this treatment, only live changing data does.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest in the dark theme. Depth comes from tonal steps and hairlines, never shadows.

dos: Do use zero corner radius on every surface, card, button, and input, without exception.
Do reserve Signal Orange (#FF4A1C) for exactly one action per screen.
Do render every live number (weight, reps, RPE, timer, 1RM) in tabular-nums Spline Sans Mono.
Do give the primary action button the 45-degree-clipped top-right corner.
Do state a plain-language reason for every prescribed number, and promote that reason to a full-bordered block for the exercise the user is doing right now.
Do say plainly when a session went backwards. Never dress up a regression.
donts: Don't use gradients, glassmorphism, or drop shadows anywhere in the dark (canonical) theme.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as an accent on any card, row, or callout, promote with a full border instead, never a side-stripe.
Don't use badge/streak-shaming, gamified motivational copy, or fitness-influencer hype language.
Don't use more than one accent-colored element on a single screen at once.
Don't fake praise or soften a regression to spare feelings.
Don't round any corner, anywhere, for any reason.
